title: Supported Image Formats
text: Supported Image Formats Uniface provides a wide range of filters that transparently support many image formats. Loading an image that has been stored in a supported format causes it to appear, whatever the runtime graphical environment. The image filters only work with bitmap-oriented image formats. Vector-oriented mark-up languages such as PostScript and HP-GL are not supported. The image filters recognize the format of the image to be loaded from the file extension, or, if that is not available, the header information of the image data. The image filters in Uniface only work in one direction. They can load any of the supported image formats and convert it to a proprietary format that can be displayed on all platforms. They cannot convert one format to another, or export from the Uniface format to another format. Search: Column Format Description Filter results... 22 rows found Table 1. Supported Image Formats Format Description Filter 22 rows found Filter 22 rows found Format Description BAF Microsoft Windows or OS/2 Array file BMP Microsoft Windows or OS/2 Bitmap file DIB Microsoft Windows or OS/2 Dibmap file GEM Digital Research (Novell) GIF CompuServe Inc. ICO Microsoft Windows or OS/2 Icon IFF Electronic Arts (IFF/LBM) JPG JPEG format MAC Macintosh MacPaint Monochrome PBM Poskanzer Portable PCX Zsoft PIC Pictor/PcPaint PICT QuickDraw Pict (Macintosh) PNG Portable Network Graphic (with some restrictions). The following features are not supported: Gamma corrections Textual support Ancillary chunks Transparency in the Tree widget Dimming on disabled controls and widgets SGI Silicon Graphics SUN Sun Raster TGA Truevision Targa TIF Aldus/Microsoft Tiff UNI Uniface internal storage format (used with glyphs) XBM X-Windows Bitmap (monochrome) XPM X-Windows Picture Bitmap (color) XWD X-Windows Dump Was this topic helpful?
